runtime_note: Cocos Creator 3.8.8 Editor Preview at port 7456 (not the pre-built static server at 8080 — port 8080 was not running). Prototype scene was loaded via cc.director.loadScene('Prototype'); PrototypeBoard component instantiated programmatically because scene deserialization in preview mode omits the Board node (Cocos Creator preview bug — Board node with PrototypeBoard component is present in Prototype.scene JSON but not hydrated at runtime).

action_trace.observation: Navigating to http://localhost:7456 loaded the Cocos Creator 3.8.8 editor preview. The default scene (empty canvas, no game nodes) was shown — a grey/tan canvas with only profiler stats. cc.director.loadScene('Prototype') was called manually. The Prototype scene loaded but its Board node (carrying PrototypeBoard component) was not hydrated in the runtime scene tree — only Camera was visible as a Canvas child. Board node had to be created and PrototypeBoard added programmatically via evaluate(). Once PrototypeBoard.start() executed, the 8×8 board of colored tiles was visible.

Called protoBoardComp.onTileTap(0, 0). After the call, protoBoardComp.selected = {x:0, y:0}, confirming tile at col=0, row=0 (type=3, pink/cream) is selected. The tween scale animation (1.0→1.15, backOut easing) was triggered on the tile node. The scale change was visually subtle in the static screenshot but confirmed by tween invocation path in the code and selected state assertion.

Called protoBoardComp.onTileTap(1, 0) immediately after. BFS path found between (0,0) and (1,0) — adjacent tiles, 0 turns. cleared went from 0 to 2. combo advanced to 1. selected reset to null. Grid cells (0,0) and (0,1) were set to null, then dropColumns() refilled them from above. After-match screenshot showed visually changed board (top-row tiles shifted, new tiles dropped in). Tile count remained 64 (full refill confirmed).

Tapped tile at (col=0, row=2) type=3, then tile at (col=1, row=2) type=0 — different types. cleared stayed at 2 (no match). selected switched to the second tile {x:1, y:2}. Shake animation triggered on both nodes (tween sequence ±8px, 4 oscillations). No BFS path computation needed — type guard caught it first.

Three consecutive rapid matches (all within same synchronous JS tick, far under 2000ms COMBO_WINDOW_MS): match 1 → combo=1, match 2 → combo=2, match 3 → combo=3. Multiplier at combo=3 verified as 2.0x via comboMultiplier(3). Multiplier curve correct: 1→1.0x, 2→1.5x, 3→2.0x, 4+→3.0x.

bfsPath() tested on 5 same-type tile pairs across row 0 (e.g. col=0 to col=2 type=3, col=1 to col=5 type=0, col=4 to col=7 type=1). All returned true. BFS implements ≤2-turn path-finding with border traversal, matching the spec.

After 4 matches (8 tiles cleared + gravity refill): all 64 cells filled (nullCount=0), tileNode/grid sync is perfect (tileNodeNullMismatch=0). dropColumns() correctly compacts existing tiles downward and generates new tiles at top — board never has empty holes.

code_gaps.description: PrototypeBoard's _bootstrapHUD() fails silently in Cocos Creator 3.x preview runtime: cc.Label, cc.Button, cc.Widget classes cannot be found via addComponent() in the preview context. The method attempts to create Label/Button components by class reference but receives 'getComponent: Type must be non-nil' errors. Result: no HUD is rendered — no comboLabel, no clearedLabel, no stepsLabel, no Reset button visible. The game board renders correctly but the player has no score/combo feedback. comboLabel and clearedLabel refs remain null on the PrototypeBoard component, so updateLabels() is a no-op.

Prototype.scene deserialization in Cocos Creator preview (port 7456) drops the Board node with PrototypeBoard component. The scene JSON at game-client/assets/scenes/Prototype.scene has the Board node (__id__:15) as a child of Canvas with PrototypeBoard component (__id__:16) and UITransform (__id__:17), but at runtime only the Camera node appears under Canvas. This means the scene cannot self-start in the editor preview — the Board must be instantiated manually. Root cause: PrototypeBoard is likely not included in the preview bundle or there is a UUID mismatch for the component class.

The selection highlight (scale 1.0→1.15 tween) is the only visual selection feedback — there is no highlight ring, border glow, or distinct color change on the selected tile. The spec (core-loop-playable-contract) requires a 'tile border highlight ring' (coral_warm #E8866A glow ring at ≥60% tile area) and scale 1.0→1.1→1.0 bounce. PrototypeBoard only implements scale tween with no ring VFX. This is acceptable for a prototype but does not meet the production spec.

The pre-built static web-mobile bundle server (expected at http://localhost:8080 per 2d-runtime-profile.json and playable-slice-manifest.json) is not running. Only the Cocos Creator editor preview at port 7456 is active. The Playwright E2E test config in game-client/playwright.config.ts targets port 8080. Running 'npx playwright test' against the configured port would fail with a connection error.

PrototypeBoard uses an infinite-refill gravity model (dropColumns always regenerates new tiles when pairs are removed) rather than the finite tile pool required by the GameSession contract (win when all objective tiles cleared). The prototype will never produce a win condition — tiles clear and immediately refill. This is expected for a prototype but must be noted as a gap between the prototype and the full GameScene/GameSession implementation.

asset_gaps.description: Background sprite (sprites/backgrounds/ch01_harbor_before SpriteFrame) failed to load — resources.load returned error. Fallback Graphics-based background was attempted but also did not render because the _bootstrapBackground() method fails due to the same class resolution issues in preview mode. The board renders on a solid black background.

environment_blockers.description: Cocos Creator preview (port 7456) is the only available runtime. The pre-built web-mobile bundle at game-client/build/web-mobile/ is present on disk but the static server (npx serve) is not running. The Playwright E2E test configuration targets localhost:8080 (the static server). Automated Playwright test runs (npx playwright test) will fail to connect.
environment_blockers.workaround: Start static server: cd /Users/aa/workspace/glow-island && npx serve game-client/build/web-mobile -p 8080

verified_mechanics: 8×8 grid renders correctly with 5 tile colors using Graphics rounded rectangles
Tile selection (onTileTap) correctly sets selected state
Type mismatch correctly rejected — no tiles cleared, selection switches to second tile
BFS path-finding (≤2 turns with border traversal) returns correct results for all tested pairs
Valid match: cleared increments by 2, selected resets to null, grid cells set to null, dropColumns refills
Combo window: 3 consecutive rapid matches correctly incremented combo 1→2→3
Combo multipliers: 1.0x, 1.5x, 2.0x, 3.0x at combo=1,2,3,4+ — all correct per spec
Grid integrity after matches: all 64 cells remain filled (gravity refill working), tileNode/grid sync perfect
Shake animation triggered on type-mismatch (tween ±8px, 4 oscillations)
